resources.name: Panama City Rescue Mission
resources.description: Emergency shelter, transitional housing, and the Power to Choose program for men. Accepts returning citizens. Provides meals, job readiness, and case management.

resources.name: Salvation Army — Transitional Housing
resources.description: Eight two-bedroom transitional apartments for families experiencing homelessness. Up to 6-month stay with case management, budgeting skills, and self-sufficiency programming.

resources.name: Doorways of Northwest Florida
resources.description: Lead agency for the Northwest Florida Continuum of Care. Coordinates housing assistance, rental and utility deposits, past-due rent help, and case management across Bay and surrounding counties.

resources.name: St. Barnabas House — Catholic Charities
resources.description: Safe transitional housing for individuals and families who are homeless or at risk. Assists with path to self-sufficiency and permanent housing placement.

resources.name: Panama City Housing Authority
resources.description: Provides 450 affordable apartment homes, 416 Housing Choice Vouchers (Section 8), and 60 VASH vouchers for veterans. Apply early — waitlists exist. Some felony restrictions apply.

resources.name: CareerSource Gulf Coast
resources.description: Free job placement, resume help, career guidance, workshops, and computer access. Covers Bay, Franklin, and Gulf counties. Ask about the HIRES Reentry Program for returning citizens.
resources.address: Gulf Coast State College, 5230 W Hwy 98, Panama City, FL 32401

resources.name: Haney Technical Center
resources.description: Vocational and technical training in trades including construction, healthcare, IT, culinary arts, and more. Partnered with CareerSource for scholarship funding.

resources.name: Gulf Coast State College
resources.description: Public college offering degrees, certificates, and workforce training. Second Chance Pell Grants available. Ask about justice-impacted student support and FAFSA assistance.

resources.name: Legal Services of North Florida (LSNF)
resources.description: Free civil legal services for low-income residents. Covers housing, employment, benefits, civil rights, and reentry barriers including expungement assistance.

resources.name: Bay County Public Defender's Office
resources.description: If you are charged with a new offense or face a probation violation hearing, request a public defender immediately at your first appearance. Free for those who qualify financially.

resources.name: Bay County Clerk of Courts
resources.description: Request your complete financial obligation summary — fines, court costs, restitution. Handles court cost modification petitions (Fla. Stat. § 938.30) to convert fees to community service.

resources.name: Florida Rights Restoration Coalition (FRRC)
resources.description: Statewide organization helping returning citizens restore voting rights under Amendment 4. Free help checking LFO eligibility and navigating the restoration process remotely.

resources.name: St. Andrew Christian Care Center
resources.description: Free food and clothing for Bay County residents. No income verification required. One of the most accessible food programs in Panama City for returning citizens.

resources.name: Catholic Charities of Northwest Florida — Food Pantry
resources.description: Food pantry serving Bay and surrounding counties. Also helps with SNAP application assistance and other basic needs referrals.

resources.name: ACCESS Florida — SNAP & Medicaid
resources.description: Apply online for food stamps (SNAP) and Medicaid. Apply immediately after release — waiting periods exist. Drug felony convictions may affect SNAP eligibility.

resources.name: Centerstone (Behavioral Health)
resources.description: Comprehensive mental health and substance abuse treatment for Bay County. Outpatient counseling, medication management, crisis services, and reentry-specific support. Sliding scale fees, Medicaid accepted.
resources.address: Multiple locations in Panama City, FL

resources.name: SAMHSA National Helpline
resources.description: Free, confidential 24/7 treatment referrals for substance use and mental health. Will connect you to local Bay County providers.

resources.name: Crisis Text Line
resources.description: Free, confidential mental health crisis support 24/7 by text. No phone call required — ideal when you need help discreetly.

resources.name: Bay Town Trolley (Bay County Transit)
resources.description: Public bus system serving Panama City, Lynn Haven, Callaway, and Springfield. Fixed routes and demand-response service. Essential for PO appointments, job interviews, and services without a car.

resources.name: Bay County DHSMV — Driver's License Office
resources.description: Restore or obtain your Florida Driver's License or State ID. Check suspension status before visiting. FDC releasees may qualify for a fee waiver.

resources.name: Bay County DHSMV — State ID
resources.description: Obtain a Florida State ID or restore your Driver's License. Bring birth certificate, Social Security card, and proof of Florida address. FDC releasees — ask about the fee waiver.

resources.name: Social Security Administration — Panama City
resources.description: Replace a lost Social Security card. Free — limit 3 per year. Required for most ID applications, job applications, and benefit enrollment.

resources.name: Florida Vital Statistics — Birth Certificate
resources.description: Order your Florida birth certificate online or by mail. Required for State ID and most document applications. $9 standard fee.

resources.name: Bay County Veterans Services
resources.description: Free assistance for veterans obtaining DD-214 discharge records, VA ID cards, benefits enrollment, and VASH housing vouchers. If you served, these benefits are yours regardless of conviction.
resources.address: Bay County Courthouse, Panama City, FL

resources.name: Broward Reentry Coalition (SaferBroward)
resources.description: Alliance coordinating reentry services across Broward County. Connects returning citizens to housing, employment, and social services. First call for navigating Broward resources.

resources.name: Faith Farm Ministries
resources.description: Residential faith-based program for men dealing with addiction and reentry. Transitional housing, work program, substance abuse treatment, and life skills. One of Broward's longest-running reentry programs.

resources.name: TaskForce for Ending Homelessness
resources.description: Street outreach, coordinated shelter entry, and housing navigation for Broward County. Entry to most emergency shelters runs through this coordinated system. Call before walking up to a shelter.

resources.name: Broward County Housing Authority (Section 8)
resources.description: Manages Housing Choice Vouchers (Section 8) and HUD-certified housing counseling for Broward County. Some felony restrictions — inquire directly about your situation.
resources.address: 4780 N State Rd 7, Lauderdale Lakes, FL 33319

resources.name: CareerSource Broward
resources.description: Free career services, job placement, resume help, and skills training across Broward. Computers and printers available. Ask about reentry programs and WOTC employer incentives.
resources.address: 2890 W Broward Blvd, Fort Lauderdale, FL 33312

resources.name: Chainless Change (HELP Program)
resources.description: Helping Ex-Offenders Live Productively — computer skills, workforce readiness, and employment placement specifically for returning citizens in Broward County.
resources.address: 4300 N University Dr Suite B204, Lauderhill, FL 33351

resources.name: BSO Day Reporting & Reentry Division
resources.description: Broward Sheriff's Office program for returning citizens 18+. Employment assistance, substance abuse programs, and case management for those at risk of recidivism.

resources.name: Legal Aid Service of Broward County
resources.description: Free civil legal services for low-income Broward residents. Covers housing, employment, benefits, and reentry collateral consequences.

resources.name: Florida Justice Center (FLJC)
resources.description: Florida's only legal aid org dedicated to justice-involved individuals. Free and low-cost legal services removing barriers from the criminal justice system — expungement, employment, housing.
resources.address: 2598 E Sunrise Blvd Suite 2104, Fort Lauderdale, FL 33304

resources.name: Broward County Public Defender
resources.description: Free legal representation for those facing new charges or probation violations who cannot afford an attorney. Request at your first court appearance.

resources.name: Feeding South Florida
resources.description: Regional food bank serving Broward, Miami-Dade, Palm Beach, and Monroe counties. Use their agency locator to find the nearest food pantry or mobile distribution in Broward.

resources.name: Henderson Behavioral Health
resources.description: One of South Florida's largest behavioral health providers. Outpatient mental health, substance abuse treatment, crisis services, and reentry support across Broward. Medicaid accepted.

resources.name: NAMI Broward County
resources.description: Free mental health support groups, education, and peer recovery programs for returning citizens and their families. No insurance required for support groups.

resources.name: Broward County Transit (BCT)
resources.description: Public bus system covering all of Broward County with 43 routes. Essential for PO appointments, job interviews, and services. Reduced fare programs available.

resources.name: Social Security Administration — Fort Lauderdale
resources.description: Replace a lost Social Security card. Free — limit 3 per year. Required for most ID, employment, and benefit applications.
resources.address: 299 E Broward Blvd, Fort Lauderdale, FL 33301

resources.name: The Lord's Place — Reentry Program
resources.description: Post-release reentry with an 83% success rate avoiding re-arrest. Case management, transitional housing, employment support, and life skills. One of the most effective programs in South Florida.
resources.address: 2808 N Australian Ave, West Palm Beach, FL 33407

resources.name: Gulfstream Goodwill Industries
resources.description: Transitional housing and employment services for returning citizens in Palm Beach County. Connected to the county's nationally recognized reentry collaborative.
resources.address: West Palm Beach area, Palm Beach County

resources.name: Palm Beach County Justice Services — Reentry
resources.description: County reentry program serving 1,200 returning citizens annually. Nationally recognized with an 11% recidivism rate. Connects to employment, housing, substance abuse, and case management.
resources.address: 205 N Dixie Hwy Suite 5.1130, West Palm Beach, FL 33401

resources.name: CareerSource Palm Beach County
resources.description: Free job placement, career guidance, resume assistance, and skills training. Ask about reentry-focused programs and WOTC employer incentives.
resources.address: 1951 N Military Trail, West Palm Beach, FL 33409

resources.name: Legal Aid Society of Palm Beach County
resources.description: Free civil legal services for low-income Palm Beach County residents. Covers housing, employment barriers, benefits, family issues, and collateral consequences including expungement guidance.
resources.address: 423 Fern St Suite 200, West Palm Beach, FL 33401

resources.name: Palm Beach County Public Defender — 15th Circuit
resources.description: Free legal representation for new charges or probation violations for those who cannot afford private counsel. Request at your first court appearance.

resources.name: Feeding South Florida — Palm Beach
resources.description: Regional food bank with pantry network across Palm Beach County. Use their agency locator to find the closest food distribution site.

resources.name: NAMI Palm Beach County
resources.description: Free peer support groups, mental health education, and recovery resources. No insurance required for support groups. Serves returning citizens and their families.

resources.name: Palm Tran (Palm Beach County Transit)
resources.description: Public bus service across Palm Beach County. Essential for PO check-ins, job interviews, and services. Low-income fare assistance programs available.

resources.name: Social Security Administration — West Palm Beach
resources.description: Replace a lost Social Security card. Free — limit 3 per year. Required for most ID, employment, and benefit applications.
resources.address: 301 Clematis St, West Palm Beach, FL 33401

resources.name: Camillus House
resources.description: Miami's oldest and largest social services organization. Emergency shelter, transitional housing, substance abuse treatment, and reentry services. Accepts returning citizens. Housing First model.

resources.name: Lotus House Women's Shelter
resources.description: Nation's largest women's shelter. Emergency and transitional housing for women and children including those justice-impacted. Comprehensive services including job training, legal aid, and childcare.

resources.name: Miami-Dade Homeless Trust (Coordinated Entry)
resources.description: Manages coordinated entry to shelter and housing across Miami-Dade. Call before walking up to a shelter. Connects to the full county housing continuum including rapid rehousing and transitional programs.

resources.name: Miami-Dade Housing Authority (Section 8)
resources.description: Public housing and Housing Choice Vouchers (Section 8) for Miami-Dade County. Some felony restrictions apply — call to discuss your specific situation before applying.

resources.name: CareerSource South Florida
resources.description: Free job placement, resume assistance, career counseling, and skills training for Miami-Dade residents. Multiple locations. Ask about reentry employment programs and WOTC incentives for employers.
resources.address: 7300 Corporate Center Dr Suite 500, Miami, FL 33126

resources.name: Branches — Reentry Employment Program
resources.description: Comprehensive reentry employment and life skills program in Miami-Dade. Job readiness training, placement support, financial literacy, and mentoring for returning citizens.

resources.name: Legal Services of Greater Miami
resources.description: Free civil legal services for low-income Miami-Dade residents. Covers housing, employment, benefits access, and collateral consequences of conviction including expungement assistance.

resources.name: Miami-Dade Public Defender's Office
resources.description: Free legal representation for new charges or probation violations for those who cannot afford an attorney. Request at your first court appearance. Do not answer questions without counsel present.

resources.name: Miami-Dade Clerk of Courts
resources.description: Request your financial obligation summary — fines, court costs, restitution. Handles court cost modification petitions (Fla. Stat. § 938.30). Search case records online.

resources.name: Feeding South Florida — Miami-Dade
resources.description: Regional food bank with the largest pantry network in South Florida. Use their agency locator to find the nearest food distribution in Miami-Dade County.

resources.name: Citrus Health Network
resources.description: Comprehensive behavioral health services for Miami-Dade County including outpatient mental health, substance abuse treatment, crisis stabilization, and reentry-specific support. Medicaid accepted, sliding scale available.

resources.name: NAMI Miami-Dade
resources.description: Free peer support groups, mental health education, and recovery resources for Miami-Dade returning citizens and families. No insurance required for support groups.

resources.name: Miami-Dade Transit (MDT)
resources.description: Public bus and Metrorail system covering Miami-Dade County. Extensive network essential for getting to PO appointments, court, job interviews, and services. EASY Card available at Publix and Walgreens.

resources.name: Social Security Administration — Miami
resources.description: Replace a lost Social Security card. Free — limit 3 per year. Required for most ID, employment, and benefit applications. Multiple Miami-Dade locations.
resources.address: 11401 W Flagler St, Miami, FL 33174 (West Miami location)

resources.name: Florida Vital Statistics — Birth Certificate
resources.description: Order your Florida birth certificate online or by mail. Required for State ID and most document applications. $9 standard fee. Fastest route is online.

resources.name: Miami-Dade DHSMV — Driver's License Office
resources.description: Obtain a Florida State ID or restore your Driver's License. Check suspension status before visiting. FDC releasees may qualify for a fee waiver — ask at the counter.
resources.address: Multiple locations — find nearest at flhsmv.gov

resources.name: Florida 211
resources.description: Dial 2-1-1 or text your zip code to 898-211 for immediate help with food, shelter, utilities, and crisis services. 24/7 statewide. Free call from any phone.

resources.name: ACCESS Florida — SNAP & Medicaid
resources.description: Apply online for food stamps and Medicaid. Apply immediately after release — waiting periods exist.

resources.name: SAMHSA National Helpline
resources.description: Free, confidential 24/7 treatment referrals for substance use and mental health disorders.

resources.name: Crisis Text Line
resources.description: Free, confidential mental health crisis support 24/7 by text. Text HOME to 741741.

resources.name: Florida Rights Restoration Coalition (FRRC)
resources.description: Statewide help restoring voting rights under Amendment 4. Free LFO checks and resolution assistance.

resources.name: Florida Legal Services
resources.description: Statewide civil legal help for low-income Floridians. Find your local legal aid office.

resources.name: FLHSMV — Driver License Status Check
resources.description: Check your Florida driver's license suspension status online before going to the DHSMV office.

resources.name: Florida Vital Statistics — Birth Certificate
resources.description: Order your Florida birth certificate online. $9 standard fee. Required for State ID.
